subject: Physics
question: Four vectors are listed below: A = [4] at 10°, B = [-5] at 5°, C = [-6] at 15°, D = [-6] at 6°. Which pair of the vectors is orthogonal?
explanation: Two vectors are orthogonal if their dot product is zero. Check the angles and magnitudes for perpendicularity.

subject: Physics
question: A race car is moving at a constant speed of 35 m/s. A security car was moving at a speed of 5 m/s as the race car passes by it and was accelerating at constant rate of 5 m/s². What was the speed of the security car when it took over the race car?
explanation: Use relative motion and kinematics: time to catch up, then v = u + at.

subject: Physics
question: A car starts moving from rest and its motion on a straight line is shown in the figure. What is the average velocity of the car within the first 6 seconds?
explanation: Average velocity = total displacement / total time. Calculate area under v-t graph from 0 to 6 s.
hint: Area under velocity-time graph gives displacement.

subject: Physics
question: Which one of the following statements is correct regarding the motion in a plane?
option_a: When a body moves in a horizontal circle, its velocity is constant.
option_b: When a body moves in a vertical circle, its speed is constant.
option_c: The centripetal force for a body moving in either vertical or horizontal circle is toward the center.
option_d: In projectile motion the horizontal component of the motion is uniformly accelerated motion.
explanation: Centripetal force is always directed toward the center regardless of orientation.

subject: Physics
question: A bullet is fired at an angle of 30° above the horizontal. If air resistance is neglected, what should be the bullet's horizontal acceleration (a_x) and vertical acceleration (a_y) when it reaches its maximum height?
option_a: Both a_x and a_y are zero.
option_b: a_x is 10 m/s² downward, and a_y is zero.
option_c: Both a_x and a_y are 10 m/s² downward.
option_d: a_x is zero, and a_y is 10 m/s² downward.
explanation: At maximum height, vertical velocity is zero but a_y = -g. Horizontal acceleration is always zero.

subject: Physics
question: Two students A and B, with masses m_A and m_B respectively, stand on wheeled trolleys. If student A has larger mass and pushes student B, then what can be said about their accelerations?
option_a: The acceleration of student B is less than that of student A.
option_b: Both boys will be accelerated by the same magnitude but in opposite directions.
option_c: The acceleration of student A is less than that of student B.
option_d: There would be no acceleration because the forces are equal and opposite.
explanation: By Newton's third law, forces are equal and opposite. a = F/m, so heavier A has smaller acceleration.

subject: Physics
question: Which of the following statement is correct about errors in measurement?
option_a: Experimental error is the same as mistake.
option_b: Errors make measured values same as accepted value.
option_c: Errors occur in every scientific investigation.
option_d: Errors can be avoided by using modern measurement instruments.
explanation: Errors are inherent in all measurements; they can be minimized but not completely avoided.

subject: Physics
question: What can be concluded when the number of significant figures increases?
explanation: More significant figures indicate higher precision in measurement.

subject: Physics
question: Two forces F1 = (8i + 3j) N and F2 = (4i + 6j) N are acting on an object. What is the magnitude and direction of the resultant force?
explanation: Resultant Fx = 12, Fy = 9; magnitude = 15 N, θ = tan⁻¹(9/12) ≈ 37°.

subject: Physics
question: The diagram below shows a path taken by a flying bird. Which one of the following vectors may represent a resultant displacement?
explanation: Resultant displacement is the straight line from start to end point.

subject: Physics
question: A body that is released from the top of a tall building reaches the ground after six seconds. What is the ratio of the kinetic energy acquired by it at the end of the first three seconds to the increment in the kinetic energy during the next three seconds?
explanation: KE = (1/2)mv², v = gt. At 3s, v=30 m/s; at 6s, v=60 m/s. Ratio calculation shows 2.

subject: Physics
question: A boy pushes a block over 12m distance within 6 seconds. The force applied by the boy varies as shown in the diagram. What is the average power loss by the boy?
explanation: Work = area under F-x graph. Average power = total work / time.

subject: Physics
question: A thin rod when rotated about an axis at the end of the rod has a moment of inertia 1/3ML². The rod has a mass of 1.5 kg and length of 1.0 m. If the rod initially at rest gains a rotational kinetic energy of 1000.0 J, at what speed does it rotate?
explanation: Rotational KE = (1/2) I ω². Solve for ω.

subject: Physics
question: A ring, a solid cylinder and a solid sphere, all having the same mass and the same radius, are held at the top of an inclined plane. If the objects roll without slipping... which one shows the order in which they will get to the bottom?
option_a: All will reach the bottom at the same time
explanation: Moment of inertia affects acceleration; sphere (smallest I) fastest, ring (largest I) slowest.

subject: Physics
question: If in a given rotational system of a body the angular momentum is increased with time, which one of the following statement is not correct?
option_a: The radius of rotation may increase
option_b: There is no torque exerted on the system
option_c: The moment of inertia may increase
option_d: There is a torque exerted on the system
explanation: τ = dL/dt. If L increases, there must be net torque.
hint: Relation between torque and angular momentum.

subject: Physics
question: Which of the following is a correct statement about the first condition of equilibrium?
option_a: The sum of all torques must be zero
option_b: Clockwise moments should balance counter clockwise moments
option_c: The net force on an object must be zero
option_d: The torque produced by a force should be perpendicular to the force
explanation: First condition of equilibrium: ΣF = 0 (translational).

subject: Physics
question: For a constant force F, if the impulse imparted to the system is large, then what can you conclude about the collision?
option_b: The contact or impact time is large
option_d: The change in velocity is small
explanation: Impulse J = F Δt = Δp. Large impulse with constant F means large Δt.

subject: Physics
question: An open trolley is rolling on a level surface without frictional loss through a vertical downpour of rain... As the trolley rolls, an appreciable amount of rainwater accumulates in the cart. What will be the speed of the cart?
option_a: It increases because of conservation of mechanical energy
option_b: It decreases because of conservation of mechanical energy
option_c: It remains the same because the raindrops are falling perpendicular...
option_d: It decreases because of conservation of momentum
explanation: Mass increases while horizontal momentum conserved → speed decreases.

subject: Physics
question: A satellite moves at a constant speed in a circular orbit about the center of the earth at an altitude half the radius of the earth above its surface. If g is the gravitational acceleration at the surface... what is the speed of the satellite?
explanation: Orbital speed v = √(GM/r), with r = 1.5R.

subject: Physics
question: An object of mass 1kg moving with a speed of 2m/s was acted on by a force that produces an acceleration of 2m/s². What is the change in kinetic energy of the object if the force is exerted on the object for 2 seconds in its direction of motion?
explanation: Final velocity v = u + at = 2 + 4 = 6 m/s. ΔKE = ½m(vf² - vi²) = 8 J.

subject: Physics
question: In the figure below, what is the speed of water as it leaves A₂? (A1=4cm², A2=12cm², V1=0.9m/s)
explanation: Continuity equation: A1V1 = A2V2 ⇒ V2 = (A1/A2)V1 = 0.3 m/s.

subject: Physics
question: An electric immersion water heater is rated at 400W. How long will it take to heat one kilogram of water from 10°C to 30°C? (specific heat of water is 4.2 J/g·K)
explanation: Q = mcΔT. Time t = Q / P.

subject: Physics
question: If the internal energy of the system decreases, then what can be concluded about the heat and the work done?
option_a: Heat is removed from the system and work is done by the system
option_b: Heat is added to the system and work is done by the system
option_c: Heat is removed from the system and work is done on the system
option_d: Heat is added to the system and work is done on the system
explanation: ΔU = Q - W. If ΔU negative, possible Q negative and/or W positive.

subject: Physics
question: If a thermodynamic system undergoes a process in which its internal energy increased by 400 J while doing 200 J of work on its surrounding, then what is the energy absorbed in or extracted from it in the form of heat?

subject: Physics
question: Which of the following statement is not correct?
option_a: A system can absorb heat from a hot reservoir and can convert it entirely into work...
option_b: The spontaneous transfer of energy from a cooler body to hotter body is quite possible.
option_c: No process is possible in which there is an overall decrease in the entropy of the universe.
option_d: The complete conversion of energy from hot source into work is not possible.
explanation: Second law: heat does not spontaneously flow from cold to hot.

subject: Physics
question: A heat engine operating between 100°C and 700°C has efficiency equal to 40% of the maximum theoretical efficiency. How much energy does this engine extract from the hot reservoir in order to do 5000 J of mechanical work?
explanation: Carnot η = 1 - Tc/Th. Actual η = 0.4 × Carnot. Then Q_h = W / η.

subject: Physics
question: From the figures given below, which one is in equilibrium?
explanation: Net force and net torque must both be zero.

subject: Physics
question: Which of the following figure indicates tensile deformation?
explanation: Tensile deformation involves pulling/stretching forces.

subject: Physics
question: A beaker with water resting on a scale weighs 40 N. A block suspended on a hanging spring weighs 20 N. The spring scale reads 15 N when a block is fully submerged... What is the reading of the scale on which the beaker with water rests...?
explanation: Buoyant force = 5 N (apparent weight loss). This force is added to the beaker scale.

subject: Physics
question: Why is an interference pattern not observed between the lights produced from a pair of car headlights?
option_a: The light sources are not collimated.
option_b: Interference is observed only in laboratory conditions
option_c: The light sources are not coherent
option_d: The spread of light as it travels out of the source
explanation: Car headlights are not coherent sources; they emit light with random phase differences.

subject: Physics
question: Suppose that three point charges are placed along a straight line and electrostatic force exerted on the middle charge is zero. What can we conclude about the charges?
option_a: The signs of the three charges should be the same
option_b: The charges at the ends must have the same sign
option_c: The middle charge should be placed at the center between the two charges
option_d: The charge at the middle and at the left end should have the same sign
explanation: For net force on middle charge to be zero, the two end charges must repel or attract it equally, requiring same sign at ends.

subject: Physics
question: Two parallel plates are kept at 3 V and 5 V... If an electron is released at O, then which way will the electron move?
explanation: Electron (negative) moves from low to high potential (towards +5V plate).
hint: Direction of force on charge in electric field.

subject: Physics
question: A capacitor of 12 µF capacitance is fully charged after it is connected between a potential difference of 9 V. How much external energy is used in charging the capacitor?

subject: Physics
question: A copper wire of cross sectional area 2 mm² carrying a current of 1.5 A. If the drift velocity of the electron is 0.00028 m/s, what is the number of charge carriers per unit volume in the copper wire?
explanation: I = n e A v_d ⇒ n = I / (e A v_d).

subject: Physics
question: Which of the following statement is correct?
option_a: Galvanometer can be converted to voltmeter... by connecting low valued resistance in parallel
option_b: Low valued internal resistance increases accuracy of voltmeter
option_c: High valued internal resistance increases accuracy of ammeter
option_d: Galvanometer can be converted to ammeter... by connecting low value shunt resistance in parallel.
explanation: Ammeter: low shunt resistance in parallel. Voltmeter: high series resistance.

subject: Physics
question: What makes the oscillatory motion a simple harmonic motion?
option_a: The acceleration... directly proportional to the velocity
option_b: The acceleration... directly proportional in magnitude but opposite in direction to the displacement
option_c: The velocity... directly proportional to the displacement
option_d: The velocity... inversely proportional to the displacement
explanation: Defining condition for SHM: a = -ω² x.

subject: Physics
question: The two end fixed string of length 0.5m has a mass per unit length 2 g/m. If the tension in the string is 80 N, what is the second harmonic frequency?

subject: Physics
question: Which one of the following is not the use of Doppler effect?
explanation: Doppler effect is used in astronomy, radar, medical imaging, but not directly in energy transmission.

subject: Physics
question: A 632.8 nm laser light produces an interference pattern on a screen of 4.0 m from a pair of slits. The slit space is equal to 0.2 mm. Calculate the fringe width.
explanation: Fringe width β = λ D / d.

subject: Physics
question: A magnetic field of strength 5 × 10^{-5} T passes through an area of 20 cm² that is at an angle of 60° to the magnetic field. What is the magnetic flux?
explanation: Φ = B A cosθ.

subject: Physics
question: An inductor has a self inductance of 12 mH. The current in the circuit decreases at a rate of 2.0 A/s. What is the magnitude of the induced electromotive force?

subject: Physics
question: Which of the following is correct about the characteristics of current and voltage in A.C circuits?
option_a: If the current leads the voltage, its capacitive reactance is greater than the inductive reactance
option_b: If the current is in phase with the voltage, the circuit is inductive
option_c: If the current leads the voltage, it is inductive circuit
option_d: If the current lags the voltage, the circuit is capacitive
explanation: In RC circuit current leads voltage; in RL current lags.

subject: Physics
question: An RLC circuit is designed to have a resistance of 400 Ω, capacitive reactance of 350 Ω and inductive reactance of 50 Ω. The circuit is connected to an alternating voltage source of effective (rms) value 50 V and frequency of 50 Hz. What is the average output power?
explanation: Average power P = I_rms² R, where I_rms = V_rms / Z.

subject: Physics
question: Which of the following has an electron volt unit?
explanation: 1 eV is a unit of energy.

subject: Physics
question: In Rutherford experiment... some charged particles deflected through a large angle... What conclusion can be drawn from this observation?
option_a: Most of the mass and all positive charge is in tiny central nucleus
option_b: Most of the atom is empty space
option_c: All the atom is positive charge
option_d: All the atom is negative charge
explanation: Large deflections indicate a small, dense, positively charged nucleus.

subject: Physics
question: In the circuit shown below, each resistor has a value of 3 Ω. What is the value of the current through R4?
explanation: Use Kirchhoff's laws or simplify the circuit to find current through R4.

subject: Physics
question: Suppose you obtain a piece of iron from your laboratory shelf. How can you experimentally determine, for sure, that it is magnetized?
option_b: If it diverges a charged electroscope
option_d: There is no single and sure way to determine its magnetization
explanation: Repulsion occurs only between like poles of magnets; attraction can occur with unmagnetized iron.

subject: Physics
question: If a charged particle enters into a uniform magnetic field perpendicular to its direction, then what will happen?
option_a: It moves in an elliptical path
option_b: The radius of its path is proportional to the magnetic field strength
option_c: The charged particle will not be affected by magnetic field
option_d: The period of the motion is inversely related to the magnetic field strength
explanation: Circular motion: T = 2πm / (qB), period inversely proportional to B.

subject: Physics
question: Two parallel wires each carrying 5 A current in the same direction are separated by 2.5 cm. What is the magnitude of the force per unit length acting on the wires? Is the force attractive or repulsive?
explanation: F/L = (μ₀ I₁ I₂) / (2π d). Parallel currents in same direction attract.
